finding: `npm run validate:git-hooks-enforcement` could exceed agent command windows without a resumable status packet or progress checkpoint, and a mixed historical-restore fixture could recurse through the expensive framework-validator lane.
expectedBehavior: Long-running validators should emit bounded progress, enforce child-process timeouts, and provide actionable evidence when a child hangs; governance blockers should fail before recursively invoking expensive validators.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed in TASK-AAO-0164: `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts` now emits per-step progress/duration, applies `ATM_VALIDATOR_CHILD_TIMEOUT_MS` (default 30s), and reports the timed-out command; `packages/cli/src/commands/hook/pre-commit.ts` skips expensive framework validators after decisive attribution/protected-state blockers. `npm run validate:git-hooks-enforcement` passed in 202s with 168 steps, and `node atm.mjs evidence run --task TASK-AAO-0164 ... --command \"npm run validate:git-hooks-enforcement\" ...` recorded fresh command-backed evidence.
followUp: Keep the progress/timeout contract covered; consider a future performance split for the 202s full suite, but do not treat a slow completed run as a hang.
